summary: Iteration 4 — auto-translate-on-load (no manual button). Backend regression 13/14 passed (1 known MyMemory long-repetitive-text edge fails). Frontend Playwright verified Board batch translate works (1 batch /api/translate call for visible titles), PostDetail+PetitionDetail title+body block-translate works (sequential translateBlock per field) with '(번역됨 / Translated)' italic label, toggle '원문 보기'↔'번역 보기' flips KO↔VI correctly, cache persisted to localStorage 'imta_translation_cache_v1' (11 keys after Board+PostDetail) and refresh used cache for cached items. SILENT FALLBACK works: testids petitions-translate-fallback, reviews-translate-fallback, comments-translate-fallback all render gray note '(번역을 불러올 수 없어 원문으로 표시됩니다)' AND original Korean remains visible. HOWEVER batch ||| translation FAILS on lists ≥~8 items because MyMemory free API drops/merges the separator: Petitions list (9 items → split_mismatch_8_vs_9), Reviews list (8 items → split_mismatch_1_vs_8), PostDetail comments (12 items → split_mismatch_11_vs_12). Users see Korean+gray note instead of translations on these three surfaces.

minor.issue: MyMemory returns empty 'translated' for 1400-char repetitive input '안녕하세요 ' × 200 — pre-existing test failure carried from iter 3; not blocking real-world usage but the 2000-char cap doc claim is generous.

integration_issues.issue: Batch ||| translate fails with split_mismatch_8_vs_9 because MyMemory ko→vi merges/drops separators across 9 segments. Fallback note shows and Korean titles render — but feature requirement (auto-translated titles) is NOT delivered.
Batch ||| translate fails with split_mismatch_1_vs_8 — MyMemory returns translation as a single block. Fallback note shows; review bodies remain Korean. review-translated-label-<id> count = 0.

Batch ||| translate fails with split_mismatch_11_vs_12 — comments-translate-fallback note shows, comment-translated-label-<id> count = 0, comment-toggle-<id> count = 0. Title+body translation (sequential translateBlock) is unaffected and works.

When a batch fails (split mismatch), nothing is cached. Every page refresh re-triggers the same /api/translate call that will fail again — wasted API quota on every navigation/refresh. Suggest caching a 'failed' sentinel with short TTL, or persisting partial successes.

issues: When batch comments fail, the gray fallback note appears ABOVE the comments list but each comment is shown without any visual hint that translation failed for that specific row — UX could pair the note with a subtle bg or icon on each card.

action_items: HIGH: Make batch translation resilient to MyMemory separator loss. Recommended: on split mismatch, automatically fall back to sequential translateBlock calls (per-item) in translateBatch — slower but reliable; or chunk into batches of 3-4 items max.
MED: Cache failed-batch sentinel with short TTL (e.g., 5 min) so refresh doesn't immediately re-hit /api/translate after a known-failing batch.
LOW: Consider lowering MyMemory load by client-side splitting long content into paragraphs and batching per-paragraph, since the free-tier behaves better on shorter inputs.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/lib/translate.js translateBatch — split regex /\s*\|\|\|\s*/ is brittle; for lists >5 items MyMemory frequently collapses separators causing total failure of the whole batch. Suggest auto-recovery: on split mismatch, do per-item fallback (Promise.all of translateBlock) instead of throwing.

/app/frontend/src/lib/useAutoTranslate.js — on failed batch, map is cleared and failed=true. There's no retry/backoff. With current MyMemory behavior, this means Petitions/Reviews/Comments effectively never auto-translate.
Frontend never reports the precise mismatch (e.g., '8 vs 9') to the user — console.warn only. Adding a debug toggle or sentry-like log would help diagnose remaining gaps.
server.py is still single-file 900+ lines (carry-over from iter 2/3); router split still recommended.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_imta_translate_batch.py (new — 6 tests: batch ||| ko→vi & ko→ja round-trip via split regex, VN user check, seed data sanity)

success_rate.backend: 100% on new batch suite (6/6); 7/8 on prior translate_anon suite (1 pre-existing long-text fail)
success_rate.frontend: Board ✓, PostDetail title+body+toggle ✓, PetitionDetail title+body+toggle ✓, Cache ✓, Fallback note ✓; Petitions list ✗, Reviews list ✗, Comments list ✗

test_credentials: session_token=test_session_1779718769701 (Cookie). 테스트 user, country_code=VN. Confirmed via GET /api/auth/me.
seed_data_creation: None new this iteration. Prior TEST_ prefixed comments/petitions/reviews from iter 3 still present and pollute Reviews list count (8 review cards visible).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auto-translate-on-load feature is HALF working. Single-block flows (PostDetail title/body, PetitionDetail title/body) via translateBlock are reliable. Batch flows (Board, Petitions, Reviews, Comments) depend on MyMemory preserving the ||| separator and currently fail on lists ≥~8 items. Fallback note testids exist and render correctly. To verify fix: clear localStorage 'imta_translation_cache_v1', visit /petitions and /reviews and /board/visa/post_seed_1; expect petition-card titles in Vietnamese, review-translated-label-<id> count > 0, comment-translated-label-<id> count == number of comments, and corresponding *-translate-fallback testids ABSENT. KR-mode tested via code review only (no DB mutation performed). Known testids: title-shimmer, block-shimmer, post-translated-label, petition-translated-label, comment-translated-label-<id>, review-translated-label-<id>, toggle-original-btn ('원문 보기'/'번역 보기'), toggle-original-petition-btn, comment-toggle-<id>, review-toggle-<id>, board/petitions/reviews/post/petition/comments-translate-fallback. Cache namespace: 'imta_translation_cache_v1' object; sub-keys 'trans_post_<id>_title_<lang>', 'trans_post_<id>_body_<lang>', 'trans_<id>_<lang>' (batch).
rca_of_the_issue: RCA: translateBatch joins items with ' ||| ' and POSTs to /api/translate → server proxies to MyMemory free API → MyMemory's ko→vi/zh/ja models occasionally normalize/translate the pipe-pipe-pipe characters, returning fewer (or one) segment(s). The frontend's strict split + length-match assertion throws split_mismatch_<N>_vs_<M>, which the hook (useAutoTranslateList) catches and sets failed=true; nothing is cached. Reproduction: log in as VN user, clear localStorage, GET /petitions — every visit reproduces split_mismatch_8_vs_9 in the console. Mitigation: (a) on mismatch, retry per-item via translateBlock and merge; (b) use a numbered token separator + tolerant regex; (c) chunk batches to <=5 items to drastically reduce mismatch probability. Fallback UX (gray note + original) already works as designed.
